summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting featuring a public hearing on Zoning Case CC-2023-004 and 005. The primary matter was a preliminary development plan for a major modification to the Heritage Woods Planned Unit Development (PUD), proposing to subdivide 38 undeveloped wooded acres into up to 6 single-family lots (with 3 homes currently planned). Residents raised concerns about water drainage, water pressure, traffic, use of HOA resources, and future building standards. The board also initiated text amendments to the zoning resolution covering administrative relief, barns/storage structures as primary uses, flag lot standards, and accessory structure regulations.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve the preliminary development plan and major modification of the Heritage Woods PUD passed unanimously (Hodges-Yes, Allen-Yes, Housen-Yes)
Motion to initiate text amendments to the zoning resolution and forward to county passed unanimously (Hodges-Yes, Allen-Yes, Housen-Yes)
Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 7:08 PM passed
action_items: Applicant must submit final development plan addressing drainage, water runoff, fire hydrant locations, access road design, shared access easement, and development standards
Fire department requested plans for access roads and proposed hydrant locations at final development plan stage
Bob Rose (Administrator) to collect contact information from concerned residents and add them to email notification list for future Heritage Woods developments
Zoning staff to post Heritage Woods updates on Facebook and township website to notify residents of future meetings
Text amendments to be forwarded to Clermont County Planning Commission and then back to Zoning Commission for review
Township staff working to pull water flow/line maps for the Heritage Woods area
key_amounts: $11,250 per year - Heritage Woods HOA annual budget paid by 75 homeowners
38 acres - undeveloped land proposed for subdivision
Up to 6 single-family lots proposed (minimum 5 acres each)
75 existing homes in Heritage Woods subdivision
3,000-4,000 square feet - anticipated home size from prospective buyer Doug Wilson
Minimum 2 acres lot size required for barns/storage structures as primary use
75 feet minimum setback from street for barns/storage structures
25 feet minimum width for flag lot pole portion
500 feet maximum length for flag lot pole portion
notable_topics: Heritage Woods PUD major modification - preliminary development plan for 38-acre subdivision into up to 6 lots
Water drainage and runoff concerns from existing residents
Water pressure concerns in Heritage Woods area
Traffic and road access concerns - single entry/exit through Heritage Woods Drive
HOA resource usage by non-HOA members (streetlights, signage, landscaping)
Future building standards and permitted uses (animals, structures) on new lots
Text amendments to zoning resolution - administrative relief process
Barns/storage structures as primary use added to use table
Flag lot dimensional requirements update to match county standards
Accessory structures in front yard in R1 district
attendees: Adam Merritt (Listing agent for property owner)
Sharon A. Hess (Resident, 1775 Heritage Woods Drive)
Doug Wilson (Prospective buyer, 5651 McCormick Trail, Milford)
Don Begley (Prospective buyer, 1097 South Muskogee Drive)
notes: The property owner/applicant was absent due to a pre-planned family vacation; the meeting had been postponed from two weeks earlier. The listing agent Adam Merritt spoke as a proxy. Chief (unnamed) gave the invocation. Heritage Woods was originally developed in the early 2000s by Mike West, who abandoned phase 3 due to access and infrastructure issues. The original PUD could have supported approximately 57 homes on the 38 acres under R1 zoning. Multiple residents expressed frustration that the decision seemed predetermined. A regular Board of Trustees meeting was scheduled to follow after a 15-minute break.
title: BOT Special 5-23-23
